title: grevillea shuttleworthiana subsp. obovata
intro: Grevillea shuttleworthiana subsp. obovata is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae, native to southern Australia. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Grevillea shuttleworthiana subsp. obovata include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create medicinal teas, syrups, and salves for topical application.
